description: The sheep: the flock animal whose fleece clothes the household. Built directly on 'mophi' (wool) — the wool-one — so a learner who knows the fleece reads the animal at sight. A counted sheep may take optional 'lipha'.
sound_symbolism: 'mophi' arrives whole and soft, and 'ra' opens it outward, the way a fleece opens under the shears. There is nothing hard in the word to snag on.
grammatical_notes: Noun: 'lo mophira mua wemo nai.' (The sheep are on the plain.) From Schleicher's fable: 'mophira mophi ma phelu.' (The sheep had no wool.) Related: 'mophi' (wool), 'wethalu' (garment).

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Wool was the pre-industrial world's renewable harvest: the animal that could be harvested without being harmed, shorn each spring and walking away whole.
